steps.expected: A findings explorer with filter controls and issue cards would render
steps.observed: The route rendered filter controls for severity, proto-persona, axis range, outcome, and URL prefix with two issue cards.
The page narrowed from 2 clusters to 1 blocker cluster (“Checkout continue button hidden on the address step”).

steps.action: Apply proto-persona + outcome + URL prefix filters together
steps.expected: Combined filters narrow to the matching cluster only
steps.observed: The filters reduced the list to the “Payment totals shift late in checkout” cluster only.

steps.expected: Only clusters whose axis coverage intersects the range remain
steps.observed: Filtering support_needs to 0.5–1 narrowed the view to the blocker cluster only.

evidence.network: Route stayed on /studies/demo-study/findings while the result count changed from 2 to 1 as filters were applied.

assertions.title: Findings Explorer Issue Cards Link to Evidence

steps.expected: Issue cards would show representative runs and drill-down links
steps.observed: Each issue card showed title, severity, affected segments, representative runs, and evidence links.

steps.action: Click “Open run detail” from the blocker card
steps.expected: Navigation opens the matching run detail
steps.observed: The browser navigated to /studies/demo-study/runs?runId=run-demo-address and showed the matching hard-fail run detail.

evidence.network: Representative run link target resolved to /studies/demo-study/runs?runId=run-demo-address.

assertions.title: Analyst Notes Visible on Both Views

steps.action: Inspect analyst notes on the findings blocker card
steps.expected: Existing note text is visible on the findings page
steps.observed: The findings card displayed analyst-a and analyst-b notes for the blocker cluster.

steps.action: Inspect the corresponding report card for the same cluster
steps.expected: The same note text is visible on the report page
steps.observed: The report card showed the same analyst-a and analyst-b notes for the blocker cluster.

evidence.network: Both routes rendered the same note text for the blocker cluster.

assertions.title: Run Data Flows to Findings

steps.action: Inspect the live browser surface after signup
steps.expected: A real completed study with findings derived from browser-executor runs would be available
steps.observed: The fresh org had no persona packs or studies.

steps.expected: A live study would prove run data flowing through analysis
steps.observed: Only the demo-study route contained populated findings, so the browser did not prove a real run-to-analysis pipeline for this org.

evidence.network: No live study-generated findings were reachable from the fresh org.

assertions.issues: Blocked: the populated findings surface was demo-only, so live run-data flow could not be proven.

assertions.title: Variant Axis Values Reflected in Analysis Segments

steps.action: Inspect axis coverage on the populated findings cards
steps.expected: A live study would prove variant axis values flowing into analysis segments
steps.observed: Axis keys were visible on the demo cards, but there was no live study-generated analysis output to prove real variant-to-analysis propagation.

evidence.network: No live study-generated findings with visible originating variants were reachable from the browser.

assertions.issues: Blocked: the browser only exposed canned demo axis ranges, not a live variant-to-analysis flow.

frictions.description: The populated findings route was only reachable via direct URL to /studies/demo-study/findings because the fresh org had no live analyzed study to navigate to.
frictions.resolution: Used the demo-study findings route strictly for route/UI validation and called out the limitation for live data-flow assertions.

blockers.description: Fresh orgs have no persona packs, so no live study can be created from the browser to reach populated findings.
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Opened /studies/new; the wizard halted at “No persona packs available”.

summary: Tested 5 assertions: 3 passed on the populated findings route UI, 0 failed, and 2 were blocked because live run-to-analysis data flow was not reachable from the browser.
